text: The patient is positioned in the totem position, and photocards are inserted. The surgical table is tilted for optimal access. The uterus is suspended using two proline sutures for better exposure. The surgeon retracts the sigmoid colon and makes a peritoneal incision at the promontorium. Medial dissection is extended towards the recto-uterine pouch to access the anterior rectal wall and posterior vaginal vault.

The surgeon suspends the uterus using two proline sutures for improved exposure. The sigmoid colon is retracted, and a peritoneal incision is made at the promontorium. Medial dissection is extended towards the recto-uterine pouch, facilitating access to the anterior rectal wall and posterior vaginal vault.

The surgeon performs sharp dissection on the lateral side of the rectum, noting the thicker parorectal tissue due to chronic prolapse. The dissection is carried out close to the rectal fascia to avoid injury to the hypogastric nerves. The rectum is mobilized, and dissection continues until the levator muscles are visible, ensuring complete mobilization for proper mesh positioning.
The surgeon continues sharp dissection on the lateral side, revealing thicker parorectal tissue, likely due to chronic prolapse.

The surgeon performs dissection close to the rectal fascia to avoid injury to the hypogastric nerves, mobilizing the rectum fully. The dissection continues until the levator muscles are visible, ensuring proper positioning for the mesh.

A trimmed two-strip purlin mesh is prepared and the first strip is fixed to the anterior rectal wall using PDS sutures. The second strip is secured to the posterior wall of the vagina in a posterior colpopexy. The surgeon then closes the peritoneum with continuous absorbable sutures, completing the procedure.

A trimmed two-strip purlin mesh is prepared outside the surgical field. The first strip is fixed to the anterior rectal wall using PDS sutures, while the second strip is secured to the posterior wall of the vagina, completing a posterior colpopexy.
The surgeon closes the peritoneum with continuous absorbable sutures, completing the procedure without complications.
